subject: Geography
title: The Caucasus Divide
description: A 60-second lesson on The Caucasus Divide.

hook.text: Mount Elbrus in the Caucasus is 5,642 meters—technically Europe's tallest peak.

buildup.text: The Caucasus Mountains sit between the Black and Caspian Seas, bridging Europe and Asia.

discovery.text: More than 50 ethnic groups live in the Caucasus, each shaped by isolated valleys.

twist.text: Whether Elbrus counts as 'European' depends on where you draw the continental line.

climax.text: The Caucasus remains one of the most linguistically diverse places on Earth.

punchline.text: Fifty cultures in one mountain range.
